nameMeanings.name: En
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 任, based on its Mandarin pronunciation, see   Ren   1.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 全, based on its pronunciation in Shanghai dialect, see   Quan   2.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 曾, see   Zeng  .

variants.name: Ren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Yong, Hong, Hui, Chuan, Gang, Guang, Jian, Liang, Lihua, Ning, Qing, Chang, Min, Shen, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 任, a term for women's rank of peerage in ancient Chinese: (i) it is said that the surname can be traced back to one of the 25 sons of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from Ren (任), the name of an ancient state (located in Renxian in Hebei province) granted to Yu Yang, said to be a son or grandson of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). His descendants adopted the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from Ren (任), the name of a state (located in Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Blanco
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Raul, Francisco, Miguel, Julio, Pedro, Jesus.
Spanish: nickname for a man with white or fair hair or a pale complexion, from blanco ‘white’.
Italian (Sicily): variant of Bianco , perhaps influenced by French blanc or Spanish blanco (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bianco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Antonio, Rocco, Aniello, Francesco, Luca, Luigi, Pasquale, Sal.

Italian: from bianco ‘white’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Old High German blanc ‘bright, shining, white, beautiful’), originally applied as a nickname for a man with white or fair hair or a pale complexion, or for someone who habitually wore white, especially in jousting or other competitions, and later used as a personal name. Compare Lo Bianco .

variants.name: Blanca
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of   Blanco   ‘white’.

nameMeanings.name: Dama
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly North American: of unknown origin, perhaps a transferred use of a surname that is fairly common in the United States. This may be of Irish origin, although the surname is not known in Ireland. Dana or Ana was the name of an ancient Irish fertility goddess, and this was also used in medieval times as a girl's name, However, it is not clear whether there is any connection between this name and the modern given name, which is sometimes also used as a feminine form of Dan or Daniel . Modern use as a boy's given name began in honour of Richard Henry Dana ( 1815–82 ), author of Two Years before the Mast, who supported the rights of fugitive slaves before and during the Civil War. The popularity of the given name was increased by the fame of the film star Dana Andrews ( 1909–92 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Dama En Blanco was born in 1141, in Spain. She had at least 1 daughter with Don Fernán Gonzáles Pantoja, Alcalde Mayor de Toledo, Patrón de la Sisla.
